question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 16, 25 and 36 are perfect squares, whereas 27 is a perfect cube.
learning_tip: Always check squares, cubes, primes and multiples first.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: A, E and I are vowels, whereas B is a consonant.
learning_tip: Group similar letters before choosing the odd one.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Rose, Lotus and Lily are flowers, whereas Potato is a vegetable.
learning_tip: Look for one item that belongs to a different group.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 13, 17 and 19 are prime numbers, whereas 21 is composite.
learning_tip: Prime number questions are common in aptitude tests.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: AB, CD and EF contain consecutive letters, whereas EG does not.
learning_tip: Observe relationships between letters.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Dog, Cat and Cow are mammals, whereas Crow is a bird.
learning_tip: Classify the given objects before answering.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 64, 125 and 216 are perfect cubes (4³, 5³, 6³), whereas 225 is a perfect square.
learning_tip: Memorize cubes up to at least 20 for placement exams.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: January, March and May have 31 days, whereas June has 30 days.
shortcut: Remember the number of days in each month.
learning_tip: Calendar-based odd-one-out questions are frequently asked in placement exams.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 49, 64 and 81 are perfect squares, whereas 72 is not.
learning_tip: Always check whether numbers are squares or cubes.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: B, D and F are even-position letters, whereas G is in an odd position.
learning_tip: Memorize odd and even alphabet positions.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Rose, Jasmine and Lotus are flowers, whereas Potato is a vegetable.
learning_tip: Group similar objects before selecting the odd one.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 23, 29 and 31 are prime numbers, whereas 33 is composite.
learning_tip: Prime numbers appear frequently in logical reasoning.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: LM, NO and PQ contain consecutive letters, whereas PR does not.
learning_tip: Observe whether letters are consecutive.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Tiger, Lion and Leopard belong to the cat family, whereas Elephant does not.
learning_tip: Animal-family questions are common in aptitude tests.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 125, 216 and 343 are perfect cubes (5³, 6³, 7³), whereas 324 is a perfect square.
learning_tip: Memorize cubes up to at least 20 for placements.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: January, March and July have 31 days, whereas June has only 30 days.
shortcut: Remember the number of days in each month.
learning_tip: Calendar-based odd-one-out questions are frequently asked in placement exams.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 121, 144 and 169 are perfect squares, whereas 172 is not.
learning_tip: Square numbers are frequently used in odd-one-out questions.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: C, F and I are 3 positions apart, whereas J breaks the pattern.
learning_tip: Observe equal spacing between letters.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Car, Bus and Train are land transport, whereas Aeroplane is air transport.
learning_tip: Identify the common characteristic first.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 14, 21 and 35 are multiples of 7, whereas 48 is not.
learning_tip: Always test divisibility by common factors.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: WX, YZ and AB contain consecutive letters, whereas AC does not.
learning_tip: Watch for wrap-around from Z to A.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Copper, Iron and Silver are metals, whereas Plastic is not.
learning_tip: Material-based classification is common in reasoning exams.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 512=8³, 729=9³ and 1000=10³ are perfect cubes, whereas 961=31² is a perfect square.
learning_tip: Know both perfect squares and cubes to solve quickly.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: Circle, Square and Triangle are two-dimensional figures, whereas Cylinder is a three-dimensional solid.
shortcut: Identify whether the figures are 2D or 3D.
learning_tip: Geometry-based odd-one-out questions are frequently asked in placement exams.

question: Find the odd one out.
explanation: 81 = 9², 256 = 16² and 625 = 25² are perfect squares, whereas 729 = 9³ is a perfect cube.
shortcut: Check whether the numbers belong to the same mathematical category before selecting the odd one.
learning_tip: One of the most frequently asked Odd One Out patterns in placement exams is distinguishing between perfect squares and perfect cubes.
